description: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 was enacted.

event: Rules 1, 2 and 17 to 21 come into force
description: Short title, definitions, and rules related to Chairperson/Members appointment, meetings, digital office, and appeal procedure come into force on publication.
Registration and obligations of Consent Manager come into force one year after publication of Rules.

event: Rules 3, 5 to 16, 22 and 23 come into force
description: Majority of substantive rules (notice, security safeguards, breach notification, verifiable consent, etc.) come into force eighteen months after publication.

event: Data Fiduciary to give notice for pre-existing consent
date: As soon as reasonably practicable after Act commencement
description: Data Fiduciary must provide notice to Data Principals who gave consent before Act commencement.

event: Intimation of personal data breach to Board (initial)
description: Data Fiduciary must immediately inform the Board of the breach.

event: Intimation of personal data breach to Board (detailed)
description: Data Fiduciary must provide updated and detailed information to the Board.

event: Intimation of personal data breach to affected Data Principals
description: Data Fiduciary must intimate each affected Data Principal.

event: Erasure for inactive accounts (specified classes) - time period
date: Three years from last approach or Rules commencement, whichever latest
description: For e-commerce entities, online gaming intermediaries, and social media intermediaries with specified user thresholds, personal data must be erased after three years of inactivity unless retention required by law.

event: Prior notice before erasure for inactive accounts
description: Data Fiduciary must inform Data Principal before erasure.

event: Minimum retention of logs and personal data
date: At least 1 year from processing date
description: Data Fiduciary must retain personal data, traffic data, and logs for a minimum of one year.

event: DPIA and audit for Significant Data Fiduciary
date: Once every 12 months from date of notification as SDF
description: Significant Data Fiduciary must undertake DPIA and audit annually.

date: Within 90 days of receipt of grievance
description: Data Fiduciary and Consent Manager must respond to grievances within 90 days.

event: Time to file appeal to Appellate Tribunal
date: Within 60 days from date of receipt of order or direction
description: Any aggrieved person may file an appeal within 60 days.

event: Disposal of appeal by Appellate Tribunal
date: Within 6 months from date of presentation
description: Appellate Tribunal shall endeavour to dispose of appeal within six months.

date: Within 6 months from receipt of intimation/complaint/reference/direction, extendable by up to 3 months at a time
description: Board must complete inquiry within six months, with possible extension.

event: Central Government to notify commencement of Act provisions
description: Different provisions may come into force on different dates as notified.

event: Central Government may exempt certain Data Fiduciaries for up to 5 years from commencement
date: Before expiry of 5 years from commencement
description: Central Government may declare that any provision shall not apply to certain Data Fiduciaries for a specified period.
